description: Public opinion shifts regarding the Keystone XL pipeline, a proposed oil transport project by TransCanada. The debate escalated between 2010 and 2015, pitting energy independence advocates against climate activists, eventually leading to President Obama's rejection of the permit in 2015.

key_events.description: State Department releases initial favorable Environmental Impact Statement.
Bill McKibben and 350.org organize massive protests at the White House.
Nebraska Supreme Court clears route, shifting focus to climate impact.
Obama officially rejects the Keystone XL permit citing climate goals.

calibration_notes: Useful for measuring the impact of organized grassroots activism on shifting public opinion away from economic framing toward climate-centric framing.
